journal_entry.rationale: Inventory is an asset account that increases when purchased. "On account" means payment is deferred, creating an accounts payable liability. The debit tuple indicates 1 asset increase (inventory), and the credit tuple indicates 1 liability increase (accounts payable). This is a standard inventory purchase on credit. No tax information was provided, so treating as pre-tax amount or tax-exempt per typical inventory purchase conventions.

output.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side. (The credit side would be accounts payable increase, but we only classify debits here.)

debit_classifier.reason: Purchasing inventory increases the Inventory asset account. This is 1 asset increase on the debit side. (The credit side would be accounts payable increase, but we only classify debits here.)

output.reason: Purchasing inventory on account means we receive inventory (asset increase on debit side) and owe the supplier (accounts payable = liability increase on credit side)

credit_classifier.reason: Purchasing inventory on account means we receive inventory (asset increase on debit side) and owe the supplier (accounts payable = liability increase on credit side)

output.reason: Entry correctly records inventory purchase on account. Balance verified: $300 debit = $300 credit. Inventory (asset) debited to increase, Accounts Payable (liability) credited to increase. Amounts match transaction text. No tax mentioned in transaction, so treating as tax-inclusive or tax-exempt is reasonable for inventory purchase. All required lines present and directionality is correct under IFRS.
